articles.title: Overnight US strikes killed Iranian soldier
articles.summary: Overnight US strikes killed Iranian soldier Overnight US strikes on Iran killed one soldier, Iranian media reported Sunday. "Lieutenant Hamidreza Dehghani, of the navy of the Islamic republic's armed forces, was martyred during the criminal, terrorist attack last night by the United States on the port of Jask" in southern Iran, the Mehr and Tasnim news agencies said, citing a local official.

articles.summary: Qatar calls Iran attacks on its territory and neighbours 'dangerous escalation' Qatar on Sunday condemned Iranian attacks against its territory and its neighbours, after Tehran launched salvos against several countries in the region. "Qatar condemns in the strongest possible terms the renewed attacks carried out by the Islamic Republic of Iran," the foreign ministry said in a statement, also referring to the targeting of Jordan, the UAE, Bahrain, Oman and Kuwait and calling the barrages a "dangerous escalation".

Iran adviser says Hormuz 'more important' than nuclear bombs
articles.summary: Iran adviser says Hormuz 'more important' than nuclear bombs An adviser to Iran's supreme leader has said that the Strait of Hormuz was more important than "dozens of atomic bombs". "This strategic passage is more important than dozens of atomic bombs, and the Islamic Republic of Iran will protect it," Mohsen Rezaee was quoted by the ISNA news agency as saying. “Hormuz acts as a strategic deterrent and Iran will protect the country’s interests and security by relying on its defence capabilities and national strength,” Rezaee also added, according to Tasnim news agency.

articles.title: Oman condemns attack on its territory
articles.summary: Oman condemns attack on its territory The Omani government has strongly condemned an attack on its territory, state media said. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it targeted Oman during a barrage aimed at countries across the Gulf. The Oman News Agency said on X that "the Sultanate of Oman affirms its condemnation and denunciation of this attack", which came just hours after the country hosted Iran's foreign minister to address security issues in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Kuwait military says responding to aerial attacks
articles.summary: Kuwait military says responding to aerial attacks Kuwait's military said its air defence forces were working to intercept an ongoing attack on its territory, as countries across the Gulf were targeted by Iranian drones and missiles.  "The General Staff of the Army notes that any explosion sounds heard are the result of air defence systems intercepting hostile attacks," the military said in a statement posted on social media.

articles.summary: One Indian national missing after attack on vessel off Oman One Indian national is missing after an attack on the commercial vessel GFS Galaxy off the coast of Oman earlier on Sunday, the Ministry of External Affairs said. "Of the 11 Indian nationals on board, 10 have been rescued so far, while one Indian national is reportedly missing," the ministry said, while condemning the attack. articles.summary: Morning recap Good morning, Middle East Eye readers. The United States launched a third round of strikes on Iran early Sunday, saying it had hit a total of 149 military targets across the country. Iran responded by claiming attacks on US military infrastructure across several Gulf states. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) also declared the Strait of Hormuz closed "until further notice" and warned that Tehran would not tolerate foreign interference in the strategic waterway. articles.title: Lindsey Graham, key Trump ally, dies after sudden illness
articles.summary: Lindsey Graham, key Trump ally, dies after sudden illness US Republican senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of Donald Trump, a staunch Israel supporter, and a foreign policy hawk who advocated for military intervention against Iran, has died from a sudden illness, his office said in the early hours of Sunday. He had just turned 71. He had served in the Senate since 2003, representing South Carolina. In May this year,  Graham threatened Saudi Arabia and other Arab and Muslim countries with joining the Abraham Accords as a price to pay for ending the Israeli-US war on Iran.

articles.summary: Hormuz escalation: Five Iranian cities hit, Qatar says three injured US forces struck five cities in Iran’s Bushehr province, including Asaluyeh, Dir, Bushehr city, Dashti and Tangestan, IRNA reported. Mohammad Mozaffari, the governor of Bushehr, told IRNA: "Following the ceasefire violation by the American-Zionist enemy, four points in this city were hit by enemy projectiles." “A total of 10 enemy projectiles hit three areas in Bushehr city and one area around the city of Choghadak, which is part of Bushehr city," he said and added the attacks were recorded between 2.45 am and 3 am local time

articles.title: UKMTO says crew of container ship struck off Omani coast rescued
articles.summary: UKMTO says crew of container ship struck off Omani coast rescued The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) said the crew of a container ship attacked off the coast of Oman earlier had been "rescued by local authorities". An Iranian missile strike on the vessel, identified by US Central Command (Centcom) as the Cyprus-flagged M/V GFS Galaxy, was cited by Washington as the trigger for a third round of US military strikes on Iran. The UKMTO had earlier reported that a fire had broken out on board the vessel and that the crew had abandoned ship in a lifeboat.

articles.summary: Israel uses land takeover orders to expand settler control, commission says Since the start of 2026, Israeli authorities have issued 49 military orders to seize 2,093 dunams (517 acres) of Palestinian land, measures Palestinian officials say are being used to expand Israeli control around settlements under the guise of security needs. The number of orders issued in the first half of 2026 has already exceeded the total recorded for all of 2025, according to a position paper released on Saturday by the Wall and Settlement Resistance Commission and cited by the Palestinian news agency Wafa. In a statement, Centcom said it had completed a third round of strikes against Iran this week, "holding Iranian forces accountable for attacking another commercial ship in the Strait of Hormuz". The IRGC claimed the attempt was "stopped by a decisive response from the navy", apparently referring to its attack on a Cyprus-flagged container ship. articles.title: IRGC hits Jordan's Prince Hassan Air Base, Qatar 'intercepts missile'
articles.summary: IRGC hits Jordan's Prince Hassan Air Base, Qatar 'intercepts missile' The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said its aerospace force had targeted US military facilities at Jordan's Prince Hassan Air Base, according to Iran's state broadcaster, IRIB. The IRGC said it fired several missiles at the base, claiming to have destroyed a command-and-control centre and hangars housing MQ-9 drones. In an update, Qatar's Ministry of Defence said its armed forces had "intercepted a missile attack which targeted" the country.
